channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Massachusetts Democrat suggests Trump’s TikTok support stems from his popularity on the app
item.description: Rep. Jake Auchincloss (D-Mass.) suggested that President-elect Trump's opposition to a TikTok ban comes from his popularity on the app and the video-sharing platform lobby’s influence on his inner circle. “With Donald Trump, it’s always some combination of two things, Jim.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they’re using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Critical warning from Microsoft: .NET install domains changing
item.description: Users who expected to be able to use the resources of the Azure content delivery network (CDN) from Edgio until November 2025, as previously announced, had a not-so-festive “gift” this week – the CDN is now shutting down on Jan. 15, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to put down your phone in 2025
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program
item.description: The DoD's big cybersecurity program advanced earlier this month. It's a big rule to carry out if it becomes effective. The post The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: The Great Tech Divide: Trump Supporters Clash Over Immigration and Industry
item.description: The internal divisions within Donald Trump's political movement have come into sharp focus with a heated online debate between tech industry leaders and hardline immigration advocates, previewing the fissures and contradictions that could emerge in his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI is a game changer for students with disabilities. Schools are still learning to harness it
item.description: Getting the latest technology into the hands of students with disabilities is a priority for the U.S. Education Department.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity
item.description: On its latest deployment, the Nimitz-class carrier went from slow military satellite uplinks to modern capabilities that move terabytes of data each day. The post Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: DC air defense gets major upgrade
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sign PDFs easily in Preview on Apple devices
item.description: With electronic signatures becoming more common, Kurt the CyberGuy explains how to produce your digital John Hancock for online documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An ex-OpenAI exec and futurist talks about AI in 2025 and beyond
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber
item.description: From Volt Typhoon to Salt Typhoon, major cyber incidents in 2024 shined a spotlight on how agencies are managing cyber threats to critical infrastructure. The post 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Nikki Haley rips Ramaswamy: 'Nothing wrong' with American culture
item.description: Former United Nations Ambassador Nikki Haley slammed her onetime GOP presidential primary opponent Vivek Ramaswamy on Thursday for arguing American culture is to blame for a lack of U.S.-born engineers.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: YouTube TV price hike got you down? 5 free alternatives
item.description: Was YouTube TV’s recent price increase the straw that broke the camel’s back for you? When does the cost of streaming TV stop going up? When?!! The answer is: Never. These streaming services are going to keep creeping upward in cost until we’re all shelling out $300 a month. Enough is enough.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Trump brings Silicon Valley to Washington
item.description: Silicon Valley is coming to Washington in force, as tech leaders take on prominent roles — both official and unofficial — in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: 6 ways to turn your IT help desk into a strategic asset
item.description: The IT help desk is often considered the “dungeon” department in most IT organizations: a place where those who can’t excel at anything more meaningful in IT are left to linger, or those who are just starting out in IT must prove their mettle before launching their “true” IT careers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Homeland Security launches internal AI chatbot for 19,000 staff
item.description: The tool, called DHSChat, will be available to approximately 19,000 employees at DHS headquarters and select pilot users across ten operating agencies.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: The greatest keyboard never sold
item.description: Even as the latest phones and wearables tout speech recognition with unprecedented accuracy and spatial computing products flirt with replacing tablets and laptops, physical keyboards remain beloved for their efficiency. Earlier this year, for example, sci-fi novelist Robert J.
item.dc:creator: Ross Rubin, Fast Company

item.title: Navy looking at cloud as GFE to reduce CMMC burden
item.description: Jane Rathbun, the Department of the Navy’s CIO, said tools from NSA and NCIS along with a new initiative could help small firms meet CMMC requirements. The post Navy looking at cloud as GFE to reduce CMMC burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
